key_papers.abstract_snippet: Fabry disease is the second most frequent lysosomal storage disorder. It is a X-linked genetic disease secondary to alpha-galactosidase A enzyme deficiency. key_papers.abstract_snippet: Fabry disease (FD) is an X-linked hereditary defect of glycosphingolipid storage caused by mutations in the gene encoding the lysosomal hydrolase α-galactosidase A (GLA, α-gal A).
